Patch Set 1:

> No, the problem is even function is not exported, but it is still
 > decalred in include/mm.h, so we need rename it otherwise could be
 > redeclare errors.

Actually, I think that would be a benefit?  If the function prototype doesn't match and we get a compile error that would be good.  Otherwise, we may be calling a function that has a different prototype but there are no compiler errors since it is just typecasting a void function pointer to the static cfs_account_page_dirtied prototype that we have declared.

commit.subject: LU-13288 llite: Find account_page_dirtied on module init
commit.message:
LU-13288 llite: Find account_page_dirtied on module init

Kernel v5.2-5678-gac1c3e4 no longer exports account_page_dirtied
Use kallsyms_lookup_name to find and use it as
vvp_account_page_dirtied on module init to avoid any
performance regressions due to symbol_get.
